title: Buttondown vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown and Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool fits your needs.
h1: Buttondown vs Campaign Monitor: Detailed Comparison
intro: Buttondown and Campaign Monitor are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. Buttondown caters to developers and technical writers prioritizing simplicity and markdown support, while Campaign Monitor targets agencies managing multiple brands with advanced design and segmentation tools. This comparison will help you decide which platform aligns with your business needs.

comparison_table.tool_b: Journey designer, smart segments, transactional email

choose_a_if: You prefer writing newsletters in markdown and want a developer-friendly platform
You're starting small and need a free tier for up to 100 subscribers
You value simplicity and a straightforward interface over complex design tools
You want affordable paid plans starting at just $9/month
choose_b_if: You're an agency or manage multiple brands and need multi-brand management features
You require advanced design capabilities with drag-and-drop template builders
You need journey designer, smart segmentation, and transactional email features
You're willing to invest in a platform with established enterprise features

faq.q: Is Buttondown better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your needs. Buttondown is better for developers and markdown enthusiasts with higher user ratings (4.8 vs 4.1 on G2), while Campaign Monitor is better for agencies needing advanced design and multi-brand management. Buttondown offers superior value with a free tier, making it ideal for startups.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Buttondown is significantly cheaper, offering a free tier and starting at just $9/month for paid plans. Campaign Monitor requires a paid subscription starting at $11/month with no free tier option. For small publishers and startups, Buttondown provides better cost efficiency.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality for your subscriber lists and content. However, you'll need to rebuild your email designs in Buttondown's markdown editor if you're using Campaign Monitor's visual builder. The transition is straightforward but may require adjusting your workflow to markdown-based writing.
